name: Cocoa Cream Filled Sponge Cake
recipeInstructions:
COCOA CREAM:
1/2 cup sugar
1/4 cup cocoa (sifted)
1 cup whipping cream (cold)
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
OVEN: 325°F
Ungreased 10-inch tube pan
CAKE:
In a large mixer bowl, beat egg whites with salt on MEDIUM speed of an electric
mixer until soft peaks form. Gradually add 1 cup sugar, beating on HIGH speed
until stiff peaks form. Set aside.
In a small mixer bowl, beat egg yolks until blended. Gradually add remaining
1/4 cup sugar. Continue beating until thick and lemon colored, about 3 minutes.
Add water and orange juice; blend well. Gradually add egg yolk mixture to egg
whites, folding with a rubber spatula until blended.
In a sieve, place cocoa, cake meal, and potato starch. Sift over egg mixture,
gently folding until blended. Fold in almonds.
Pour batter into pan. Bake for 50-55 minutes or until the cake springs back
when lightly touched near the center. Immediately invert pan on a heatproof funnel or bottle. Cool completely.
Carefully run knife along sides of pan to loosen cake; remove from pan.
With a long serrated knife, split cake horizontally into thirds. Spread preserves between layers. Garnish with a generous spoonful of COCOA CREAM.
COCOA CREAM:
In a small mixer bowl, stir together sugar and cocoa. Sift together. Add whipping cream and vanilla extract. Beat until slightly stiffened. Makes about 2 cups.
